items.title: Replace the raw-ADMIN_KEY-to-browser flow with a short-lived signed token
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** new `functions/_lib/auth.js` issues/verifies HMAC-SHA256 tokens. `/api/admin-key` now returns a short-lived signed token (TTL `ADMIN_TOKEN_TTL_SEC`, default 2h; signed with `TOKEN_SECRET`→ falls back to `ADMIN_KEY`) instead of the raw key — the raw `ADMIN_KEY` never reaches the browser. The admin page stores/sends the token (x-admin-key header for writes; `bb_staging` cookie for staging launch). `/api/status`, `/api/config`, and the `_middleware` staging gate verify it via `isAdminAuthorized()`; the raw key stays accepted server-side as a fallback (never handed to a browser). Tokens expire. Verified with a Node WebCrypto test (valid/tamper/expiry/fallback). Full E2E needs the deployed Access+KV env.

items.title: Verify the Cloudflare Access JWT signature (defense-in-depth)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `/api/admin-key` verifies the `Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion` RS256 signature against the team JWKS (`<ACCESS_TEAM_DOMAIN>/cdn-cgi/access/certs`, cached 1h) plus audience (`ACCESS_AUD`), issuer, and expiry before issuing a token (`verifyAccessJwt` in `functions/_lib/auth.js`). When `ACCESS_TEAM_DOMAIN`/`ACCESS_AUD` are unset it falls back to the prior header-presence check (route is still behind Access + the middleware gate). Verified with a Node WebCrypto test using a generated RSA keypair + mocked JWKS: a genuine token is accepted; forged-signature, absent, expired, wrong-aud, wrong-iss, and unknown-kid are all rejected. Full E2E needs the deployed Access env.

items.title: (staging) Design review: per-day vs per-trade notes & data model
items.doneNote: Design review of how per-DAY and per-TRADE notes fit together. FINDINGS: per-day notes (JOURNAL store, {date,text,updated}) are plain text only, captured in a bare textarea under the calendar; per-trade notes (TRADEMETA store, {id,tags,note,shots,updated}) are rich — tags + note + downscaled screenshots — captured in a modal editor in Manage data. The asymmetry is backwards: the DAY is the natural home for a chart screenshot / morning plan / 'tilted' tag, yet only trades can hold them. Two storage shapes + two capture surfaces mean any future notes feature (search, export, tag filter) must be built twice. The 'note'-only Day-notes list in Manage data is ALREADY fixed — verified in-browser it previews date + full text (newest first); the only bare literal 'note' left is the compact per-trade presence chip (metaChips), which is intentional. RECOMMENDATION: one annotation shape {tags,text,shots}, two scopes (day|trade). Low-risk path = extend the JOURNAL record to {date,text,tags,shots,updated} (old {date,text} records read fine, fields default empty; additive export), reuse the trade editor's tag input + screenshot downscale pipeline, and replace the bare calendar textarea with a shared annotation editor (quick textarea that expands to full editor). Keep the CH11 capture-the-date autosave and the SHOT downscale pipeline. Spun off F16 (unify: give day notes tags + screenshots), CH19 (extract a shared annotation-editor component), CH20 (Manage-data Day-notes rows open/edit + consistent note·img·tag markers). No app code shipped — R3 is review-only.

items.title: Review & plan: repo/folder structure for maintainability
items.doneNote: Reviewed the repo/folder structure against best practice for a build-stepless Cloudflare Pages static site. KEY FINDING: because there is NO build step, the repo root IS the deploy/web root — the folder layout is simultaneously the public URL structure AND the deploy contract (measured coupling: 23 absolute /app/ refs, 19 /assets/ refs, /data/*.json + /api/* fetches, _redirects/_headers/robots.txt/sitemap.xml + page canonicals, and hardcoded path prefixes in build-includes ['.', 'app'], build-manifest data/* filename excludes, and bump-version's prod-shipping classifier). So the textbook '/src vs /public with a bundler' reorg FIGHTS the architecture: it either changes every public URL (canonical/sitemap/_redirects/bookmark churn) or requires a build/copy step that violates the 'no build step / deploys as static files' design pillar. CONCLUSION: keep the current root=web-root layout — it is the idiomatic, correct structure for this deploy model (functions/ pinned at root as Pages requires; _headers/_redirects/robots/sitemap/favicon at root; concerns co-located in app/ partials/ data/ assets/ scripts/ functions/; tokens+partials already single-sourced via A1/A5/A6). CONSIDERED & DECLINED as not worth the churn: scripts/->tests/ split (cosmetic; CI path coupling), assets/ -> img+js split (changes ~19 URLs + favicon/OG/_headers refs), data/ subgrouping (breaks fetch URLs + manifest keys), marketing pages -> /site (SEO/canonical/sitemap churn). The ONE Pages-compatible reorg, should the root ever genuinely need it, is a public/ output-dir split (browser-served files under public/, Pages build-output-directory=public, keep functions/+scripts/+partials/+tooling at root) — URLs stay identical, but _headers/_redirects relocate into public/ and every build-script path prefix changes; deferred (cost > payoff for a small project now). SPUN OFF: A18 (guardrail — repo layout is the deploy contract; reject a /src+bundler reorg, and if ever reorganizing use the public/ output-dir approach + update the full coupled-path set in lockstep) and CH28 (document the build-stepless layout contract + path-coupling map in docs/architecture.md). Review-only; no code shipped.

items.doneNote: Documented the build-stepless layout contract. Added a 'Repository layout & the deploy contract' section to docs/architecture.md (after Design pillars; registered in the Contents list): no build step -> the repo root IS the Cloudflare Pages web root -> a file's path IS its public URL. Lists what Pages pins at the deploy root (functions/, _headers, _redirects, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, favicon, and the /-serving pages) and a coupled-path table that must change in lockstep if any browser-served file moves: the ~23 /app/ + ~19 /assets/ absolute refs and /data//api fetches, _redirects, _headers (CSP connect-src), robots/sitemap + page canonicals (CH5/CH6), build-includes.mjs (['.', 'app'] scan + partials/), build-manifest.mjs (data/* hashing + filename excludes), and bump-version.mjs (prod-shipping path classifier). States: do NOT reorganize into src/+public/ with a bundler (violates the no-build pillar); the only Pages-compatible reorg is a public/ output-dir split. CLAUDE.md's Repo layout section gained a blockquote summary + link to the new section (refs guardrail A18). Docs only; no code/build impact.

items.doneNote: Done — analyzed and decided NOT to adopt SvelteKit; staying on the Vite multi-page + Svelte 5 SPA (ADR-001). Of SvelteKit's headline features, SSR + form actions are non-features for a local-only app, SSG + endpoints are already covered (Vite MPA static marketing pages; the functions/* edge layer), routing is a future micro-dep if ever needed, and adapter-cloudflare only exists to serve the rest. Its load/server idiom is a standing hazard to the HARD no-trade-data-leaves-the-browser pillar (the server must never see trades), and adopting it would re-open the just-stabilized A18/A26 deploy contract and churn the security-reviewed functions/* layer for zero capability gain. Owner reviewed and confirmed: not converting, not revisiting — so the written analysis doc was reverted rather than kept. Sequencing left intact: A69 (Svelte-ify marketing) proceeds with plain Svelte under the Vite MPA build, A30 (reorg) on the Vite/Svelte structure, A61 (TS) orthogonal.
items.title: Discuss: adopt SvelteKit (pros/cons + analysis)

items.doneNote: Done — read-only structure audit + decided target layout delivered in docs/structure-reorg-plan.md. Findings: the flat root-as-web-root layout no longer earns its keep (A26 decoupled source from URL; no surface is servable un-built), so the A30 reorg is justified as a P3 maintainability cleanup. Catalogued the pure-logic core, the mixed assets/ dir (bundled JS+SVG vs the one verbatim-static og-image.png), the misplaced/colliding util.js files (assets/util.js shared-core vs app/staging-svelte/util.js app-only), the app/staging-svelte misnomer, app/entitlements.js as intentional-but-currently-dead scaffold, and the full coupled-path set (vite/build-manifest/bump-version/build-includes/copy-static/jsconfig/eslint/prettier + imports). Per the owner's call, designed ONE target layout (src/{lib,app,site} + static/ publicDir; functions/ pinned at root) that ABSORBS A69 so A30 lands once and is final: src/site/ has prepared homes for the Svelte marketing components + shared chrome, retiring partials/ + build-includes; static/ replaces copy-static.mjs. Recommendation: A61(TS) decision first, then A69+A30 as one coordinated reorg-and-convert pass. Follow-ups: A30 + A69 prompts repointed at the plan; filed A76 (util.js collision + entitlements.js scaffold cleanup, rides with A30).
items.title: Audit project structure to optimize for Vite + Svelte

items.doneNote: Done — discussion + decision delivered in docs/typescript-decision.md. Owner decided: ADOPT full native TypeScript everywhere (incl. the pure-logic core), strict from the start, with the .js→.ts / lang=ts renames FOLDED INTO the A30 reorg (touch files once). Analysis covered what TS buys beyond today's JSDoc @ts-check (biggest win = real types on the 18 untyped Svelte components; ergonomics + one language elsewhere; marginal added safety on the already-@ts-check'd core), and the mechanics/risks: Vite/esbuild compiles .ts free at build; the node test suites can't run .ts raw → use Node 22 native --experimental-strip-types (zero dep, preferred) or tsx fallback; svelte-check (dev-only) added for component typing; jsconfig→tsconfig strict; functions/ convertible separately. A29 reconciled: JS→TS is a behavior-preserving syntax-host change (no logic rewrite), gated by the node suites passing identically. Follow-ups: A30 prompt updated to fold in the full-TS conversion; A29 guardrail amended with the TS clarification; filed A77 (Phase-1 toolchain scaffold pre-step, P2) and A78 (Phase-3 functions→TS, P3).
items.title: Discuss: migrate from JS to TypeScript

items.doneNote: Done (A61 Phase 1). Stood up the TS toolchain on the current tree, no file moves: replaced jsconfig.json with tsconfig.json (allowJs + checkJs:false reproduce today's opt-in @ts-check behavior; strict:false as a TRANSITION — the global strict flip happens in the A30 conversion so there's no loose-TS end state) + allowImportingTsExtensions (Node's native type-stripping runtime needs real .ts specifiers). Added svelte-check (dev-only, pinned 4.3.3) + tsconfig.svelte.json; `npm run typecheck` now runs tsc + svelte-check, so the 18 Svelte components are type-checked for the first time (0 errors, 5 warnings). Proved the .ts test runner: Node 22.22 runs/imports .ts with type-stripping ON BY DEFAULT (no flag, zero dep). Verified npm test green.

items.title: Convert the homepage, admin, and info site to Svelte
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the marketing/info site is now Svelte under the existing Vite MPA build (no SvelteKit — A62 deferred). A small build-only SSG plugin (`vite-ssg.mjs`) server-renders each page component with `svelte/server` `render()` into its committed template's `<!--ssg-outlet-->`, so every page ships as static, fully-rendered HTML (SEO + first paint) and hydrates in place — NOT behind the app SPA shell (ADR-001). Authored `Home/Howto/Roadmap/Changelog/Legal/Admin.svelte` (`src/site/components/`) + shared `Nav/Footer/SiteShell.svelte` (`src/site/lib/`) + per-page client entries (`src/site/entries/`); ported `home.js`/`changelog.js`/`admin.js` into onMount logic (Svelte auto-escaping replaced `esc()`). Folded `home.css`/`site.css`/`admin.css` into scoped component `<style>` (tokens.css stays the single source); CSP `style-src 'self'` preserved (a client-only CSSOM action sets the one dynamic backlog-bar width, matching A55). Retired `partials/{nav,footer}.html` + `scripts/build-includes.mjs` and the build-includes half of the CI drift gate; updated package.json, vite.config (ssg plugin), bump-version (classifies `src/site/**` non-admin as prod-only; admin = neither) + test-version, tsconfig.svelte/eslint globs. URLs verified 1:1 (/, /howto.html, /roadmap.html, /changelog.html, /legal.html, /admin.html); favicons/OG meta intact; admin stays Cloudflare Access–gated + noindex. e2e adds server-side SSR-content assertions (ssg.spec) + boots all six pages — npm test + build + 23 e2e green.
